Page.Width: {Binding RelativeSource={RelativeSource Mode=FindAncestor, AncestorType={x:Type ContentControl}}, Path=ActualWidth, FallbackValue=700}
Page.Height: {Binding RelativeSource={RelativeSource Mode=FindAncestor, AncestorType={x:Type ContentControl}}, Path=ActualHeight, FallbackValue=700}

Run.Text: Commander, our intel sources have passed a warning of another enemy submarine operating in the area.

Using the array of bottom-laid sensors operated by the Japan Self-Defense Force, a new contact has been detected. The 'datum' for this contact is now labeled as 'Datum A01' on your Tactical Display. A 'datum' is the last known position of the contact, marked as a geographic coordinate on your map. The circle around the datum is the 'Furthest On Circle (FOC)', which is based on the last known contact and the submarine's estimated speed. In this case, Datum A01 is about 10 nautical miles across based on the age of the contact.
Datum information typically takes time to be processed by the shore station and then relayed to the ASW task group, hence the size of the Furthest On Circle.
